metadata.author: Google
metadata.comments: Solution template for Google Threat Intelligence

metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

properties.description: Google Threat Intelligence data connector with template version 3.2.3

connectorUiConfig.title: Google Threat Intelligence Relevance System Alerts
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Google Threat Intelligence (virustotal)](https://www.virustotal.com) data connector ingests GTI Relevance System Alerts into Microsoft Sentinel. The connector polls the GTI Relevance System Alerts API on a configurable schedule and stores Relevance System Alerts data in the RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL custom table, enabling security analysts to investigate threats, correlate incidents, and respond to intelligence-driven alerts within the Sentinel workspace.

sampleQueries.query:
RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL
| extend severity = coalesce(severityAnalysis_severityLevel, detail_dataLeak_severity, detail_insiderThreat_severity, detail_initialAccessBroker_severity)
| summarize count() by severity
| sort by count_ desc

RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL
| summarize count() by state
| sort by count_ desc

sampleQueries.description: High and critical priority GTI Alerts
sampleQueries.query:
RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL
| where priorityAnalysis_priorityLevel in ('PRIORITY_LEVEL_HIGH', 'PRIORITY_LEVEL_CRITICAL')
| project TimeGenerated, displayName, state, priorityAnalysis_priorityLevel, severityAnalysis_severityLevel, detail_detailType
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(30d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions on the workspace are required.

read permissions to shared keys for the workspace are required. [See the documentation to learn more about workspace keys](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/agent-windows#obtain-workspace-id-and-key).

customs.name: Azure Subscription
customs.description: An Azure Subscription with owner role is required to register an application in Microsoft Entra ID and assign the contributor role to the app in the resource group.

customs.name: Microsoft.Web/sites permissions
customs.description: Read and write permissions to Azure Functions to create a Function App is required. [See the documentation to learn more about Azure Functions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/).

customs.name: Google Threat Intelligence API Key
customs.description: A Google Threat Intelligence API key and project ID are required. See the [GTI documentation](https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/docs/how-get-gti-api-keys) to learn how to obtain an API key.

instructionSteps.description: >**NOTE:** This connector uses an Azure Function App (Timer Trigger, Python 3.12) to poll the Google Threat Intelligence Alerts API on a configurable schedule and ingest GTI Relevance System Alerts into the RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL custom table in Microsoft Sentinel. This may result in additional data ingestion costs. Check the [Azure Functions pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/functions/) for details.

**STEP 1 - Obtain Google Threat Intelligence API Credentials**

1. Log in to the [Google Threat Intelligence portal (virustotal)](https://www.virustotal.com).
2. Navigate to your GTI project settings and copy the **Project ID**.
3. Generate an **API Key** for your project and copy it securely.

**STEP 2 - App Registration steps for the Application in Microsoft Entra ID**

This integration requires an App registration in the Azure portal. Follow the steps in this section to create a new application in Microsoft Entra ID:
1. Sign in to the [Azure portal](https://portal.azure.com/).
2. Search for and select **Microsoft Entra ID**.
3. Under **Manage**, select **App registrations > New registration**.
4. Enter a display **Name** for your application (e.g. RelevanceSystemAlertsSentinelConnector).
5. Select **Register** to complete the initial app registration.
6. When registration finishes, the Azure portal displays the app registration's Overview pane. Copy the **Application (client) ID** and **Directory (Tenant) ID** — these are required as configuration parameters.

> **Reference link:** [https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app)

**STEP 3 - Add a client secret for the application in Microsoft Entra ID**

Sometimes called an application password, a client secret is a string value required for the connector. Follow the steps in this section to create a new Client Secret:
1. In the Azure portal, in **App registrations**, select your application.
2. Select **Certificates & secrets > Client secrets > New client secret**.
3. Add a description for your client secret.
4. Select an expiration for the secret or specify a custom lifetime. Limit is 24 months.
5. Select **Add**.
6. *Record the secret's value for use as the AzureClientSecret parameter. This secret value is never displayed again after you leave this page.*

> **Reference link:** [https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app#add-a-client-secret](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app#add-a-client-secret)

**STEP 4 - Get the Object ID of your application in Microsoft Entra ID**

After creating your app registration, follow the steps in this section to get the Object ID:
1. Go to **Microsoft Entra ID**.
2. Select **Enterprise applications** from the left menu.
3. Find your newly created application in the list (search by the name you provided).
4. Click on the application.
5. On the overview page, copy the **Object ID**. This is the **AzureEntraObjectID** needed for the ARM template role assignment.
**STEP 5 - Deploy the connector and the associated Azure Function App**

Use this method for automated deployment of the GTI Relevance System Alerts connector using an ARM Template.

1. Click the **Deploy to Azure** button below. 

	[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://aka.ms/sentinel-GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts-azuredeploy) [![Deploy to Azure Gov](https://aka.ms/deploytoazuregovbutton)](https://aka.ms/sentinel-GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts-azuredeploy-gov)
2. Select the preferred **Subscription**, **Resource Group**, and **Location**. 
3. Enter the below information: 

	 a. **FunctionName** - Enter a unique name for the Azure Function App (maximum 11 characters). Example: `GTIRSAlerts`

	 b. **Location** - Azure region for the Function App and supporting resources. Defaults to the resource group location. Example: `eastus`

	 c. **WorkspaceName** - Enter the Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics workspace name. Example: `MyWorkspace`

	 d. **AppInsightsWorkspaceResourceID** - Enter the fully qualified resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace for Application Insights. Format: `/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/{workspaceName}`

	 e. **AzureTenantID** - Enter the Azure Tenant (Directory) ID obtained in STEP 2. Example: `xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx`

	 f. **AzureClientID** - Enter the Application (Client) ID obtained in STEP 2. Example: `xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx`

	 g. **AzureClientSecret** - Enter the client secret value obtained in STEP 3.

	 h. **AzureEntraObjectID** - Enter the Object ID of your Microsoft Entra app obtained in STEP 4. Example: `xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx`

	 i. **GTIProjectId** - Enter the Google Threat Intelligence project ID obtained in STEP 1. Example: `my-gti-project-id`. See the [GTI documentation](https://docs.cloud.google.com/threat-intelligence/guides/get-started) to learn how to get started.

	 j. **GTIApiKey** - Enter the Google Threat Intelligence API key obtained in STEP 1.

	 k. **GTIRelevanceSystemAlertsTableName** - Enter the name of the custom table for GTI Relevance System Alerts. The `_CL` suffix is appended automatically. **Maximum 52 characters** (the `_CL` suffix counts toward the Log Analytics 57-character table name limit). Default: `RelevanceSystemAlerts`

	 l. **StartDate** - (Optional) Enter the start datetime in `yyyy-mm-ddTHH:MM:SS[.fff]Z` format. Example: `2026-05-20T15:43:51Z`. Must be a past date. If not provided, data from the last 7 days will be fetched.

	 m. **GTIFilterExpression** - (Optional) Enter a GTI API filter expression to narrow which alerts are ingested. Example: `state = "NEW"`. Any `audit.update_time` condition will be automatically removed — the connector manages that filter internally. Your expression is combined with the checkpoint filter using `AND`.

	 n. **Schedule** - Enter a valid Quartz Cron expression for the timer trigger schedule. Default: `0 */10 * * * *` (every 10 minutes).

	 o. **LogLevel** - Set the log level for the Function App. Allowed values: `Debug`, `Info`, `Warning`, `Error`. Default: `Info`.

	 p. **KeyVaultName** - Enter name of keyvault where tokens will be stored.

4. Mark the checkbox labeled **I agree to the terms and conditions stated above**. 
5. Click **Purchase** to deploy.
instructionSteps.title: Azure Resource Manager (ARM) Template

instructionSteps.description:
**STEP 6 - Create a Keyvault**

 Follow these instructions to create a new Keyvault.
 1. In the Azure portal, Go to **Key vaults** and click on Create.
 2. Select Subscription, Resource Group and provide unique name of keyvault.

**STEP 7 - Create Access Policy in Keyvault**

 Follow these instructions to create access policy in Keyvault.
 1. Go to keyvaults, select your keyvault, go to Access policies on left side panel, click on create.
 2. Select all keys & secrets permissions. Click next.
 3. In the principal section, search for the function app name which was deployed in STEP - 5. Click next.

 **Note:** Ensure the Permission model in the Access Configuration of Key Vault is set to **'Vault access policy'**

**STEP 8 - Verify Data Ingestion**

After deployment, verify that data is being ingested into Microsoft Sentinel:
1. Navigate to **Microsoft Sentinel** → **Logs**.
2. Run the following query:

```kql
RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| limit 10
```

3. Data should appear within **5–10 minutes** of the first function execution.
4. The connector status on the **Data connectors** page shows **Connected** once data has been received within the last 30 days.

**Troubleshooting**

**Connector shows Disconnected**
- Verify the Function App is running: Azure Portal → Function App → Overview → Status should be **Running**.
- Confirm data has been ingested within the last 30 days.
- Review Application Insights logs for errors.

**Authentication errors (401 / 403)**
- Verify **AzureClientID**, **AzureClientSecret**, and **AzureTenantID** are correct in Function App → Configuration → Application settings.
- Ensure the client secret has not expired.
- Confirm the App Registration has sufficient permissions.

**No data in RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL table**
- Check Function App → Functions → RelevanceSystemAlerts → Monitor for execution errors.
- Verify the GTI API key and project ID are valid.
- Confirm the source API is reachable from Azure.
- Review Application Insights traces:

```kql
traces
| where timestamp > ago(1h)
| order by timestamp desc
| take 50
```

**Rate limit errors (429)**
- The connector automatically retries with exponential backoff. If errors persist, reduce the polling frequency by increasing the **Schedule** cron interval.

properties.description: GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts Data Parser with template version 3.2.3

properties.query:
let GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts = view () {
    RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL
    | extend
        AlertId   = tostring(split(name, "/")[-1]),
        AlertName = tostring(name),
        ETag      = tostring(etag),
        State     = tostring(state),
        DisplayName   = tostring(displayName),
        FindingCount  = tostring(findingCount),
        AiSummary     = tostring(aiSummary),
        Findings      = todynamic(findings),
        AuditCreateTime  = todatetime(audit_createTime),
        AuditUpdateTime  = todatetime(audit_updateTime),
        AuditCreator     = tostring(audit_creator),
        AuditUpdater     = tostring(audit_updater),
        DetailType       = tostring(detail_detailType),
        DataLeakSeverity = tostring(detail_dataLeak_severity),
        DataLeakDiscoveryDocumentIds             = todynamic(detail_dataLeak_discoveryDocumentIds),
        InsiderThreatSeverity                    = tostring(detail_insiderThreat_severity),
        InsiderThreatDiscoveryDocumentIds        = todynamic(detail_insiderThreat_discoveryDocumentIds),
        InitialAccessBrokerSeverity              = tostring(detail_initialAccessBroker_severity),
        InitialAccessBrokerDiscoveryDocumentIds  = todynamic(detail_initialAccessBroker_discoveryDocumentIds),
        IsRelevant              = tobool(relevanceAnalysis_relevant),
        RelevanceLevel          = replace_string(tostring(relevanceAnalysis_relevanceLevel), "RELEVANCE_LEVEL_", ""),
        RelevanceConfidence     = replace_string(tostring(relevanceAnalysis_confidence), "CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_", ""),
        RelevanceReasoning      = tostring(relevanceAnalysis_reasoning),
        RelevanceEvidenceCommonThemes   = todynamic(relevanceAnalysis_evidence_commonThemes),
        RelevanceEvidenceDistinctThemes = todynamic(relevanceAnalysis_evidence_distinctThemes),
        SeverityLevel      = replace_string(tostring(severityAnalysis_severityLevel), "SEVERITY_LEVEL_", ""),
        SeverityConfidence = replace_string(tostring(severityAnalysis_confidence), "CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_", ""),
        SeverityReasoning  = tostring(severityAnalysis_reasoning),
        PriorityLevel      = replace_string(tostring(priorityAnalysis_priorityLevel), "PRIORITY_LEVEL_", ""),
        PriorityReasoning  = tostring(priorityAnalysis_reasoning)
    | project
        TimeGenerated,
        AlertId,
        AlertName,
        ETag,
        State,
        DisplayName,
        FindingCount,
        AiSummary,
        Findings,
        AuditCreateTime,
        AuditUpdateTime,
        AuditCreator,
        AuditUpdater,
        DetailType,
        DataLeakSeverity,
        DataLeakDiscoveryDocumentIds,
        InsiderThreatSeverity,
        InsiderThreatDiscoveryDocumentIds,
        InitialAccessBrokerSeverity,
        InitialAccessBrokerDiscoveryDocumentIds,
        IsRelevant,
        RelevanceLevel,
        RelevanceConfidence,
        RelevanceReasoning,
        RelevanceEvidenceCommonThemes,
        RelevanceEvidenceDistinctThemes,
        SeverityLevel,
        SeverityConfidence,
        SeverityReasoning,
        PriorityLevel,
        PriorityReasoning
};
GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts

let GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts = view () {
    RelevanceSystemAlerts_CL
    | extend
        AlertId   = tostring(split(name, "/")[-1]),
        AlertName = tostring(name),
        ETag      = tostring(etag),
        State     = tostring(state),
        DisplayName   = tostring(displayName),
        FindingCount  = tostring(findingCount),
        AiSummary     = tostring(aiSummary),
        Findings      = todynamic(findings),
        AuditCreateTime  = todatetime(audit_createTime),
        AuditUpdateTime  = todatetime(audit_updateTime),
        AuditCreator     = tostring(audit_creator),
        AuditUpdater     = tostring(audit_updater),
        DetailType       = tostring(detail_detailType),
        DataLeakSeverity = tostring(detail_dataLeak_severity),
        DataLeakDiscoveryDocumentIds             = todynamic(detail_dataLeak_discoveryDocumentIds),
        InsiderThreatSeverity                    = tostring(detail_insiderThreat_severity),
        InsiderThreatDiscoveryDocumentIds        = todynamic(detail_insiderThreat_discoveryDocumentIds),
        InitialAccessBrokerSeverity              = tostring(detail_initialAccessBroker_severity),
        InitialAccessBrokerDiscoveryDocumentIds  = todynamic(detail_initialAccessBroker_discoveryDocumentIds),
        IsRelevant              = tobool(relevanceAnalysis_relevant),
        RelevanceLevel          = replace_string(tostring(relevanceAnalysis_relevanceLevel), "RELEVANCE_LEVEL_", ""),
        RelevanceConfidence     = replace_string(tostring(relevanceAnalysis_confidence), "CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_", ""),
        RelevanceReasoning      = tostring(relevanceAnalysis_reasoning),
        RelevanceEvidenceCommonThemes   = todynamic(relevanceAnalysis_evidence_commonThemes),
        RelevanceEvidenceDistinctThemes = todynamic(relevanceAnalysis_evidence_distinctThemes),
        SeverityLevel      = replace_string(tostring(severityAnalysis_severityLevel), "SEVERITY_LEVEL_", ""),
        SeverityConfidence = replace_string(tostring(severityAnalysis_confidence), "CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_", ""),
        SeverityReasoning  = tostring(severityAnalysis_reasoning),
        PriorityLevel      = replace_string(tostring(priorityAnalysis_priorityLevel), "PRIORITY_LEVEL_", ""),
        PriorityReasoning  = tostring(priorityAnalysis_reasoning)
    | project
        TimeGenerated,
        AlertId,
        AlertName,
        ETag,
        State,
        DisplayName,
        FindingCount,
        AiSummary,
        Findings,
        AuditCreateTime,
        AuditUpdateTime,
        AuditCreator,
        AuditUpdater,
        DetailType,
        DataLeakSeverity,
        DataLeakDiscoveryDocumentIds,
        InsiderThreatSeverity,
        InsiderThreatDiscoveryDocumentIds,
        InitialAccessBrokerSeverity,
        InitialAccessBrokerDiscoveryDocumentIds,
        IsRelevant,
        RelevanceLevel,
        RelevanceConfidence,
        RelevanceReasoning,
        RelevanceEvidenceCommonThemes,
        RelevanceEvidenceDistinctThemes,
        SeverityLevel,
        SeverityConfidence,
        SeverityReasoning,
        PriorityLevel,
        PriorityReasoning
};
GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts

properties.description: ThreatHuntHash_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.2.3

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting Hash
properties.query:
_Im_FileEvent
| where isnotempty(Hash)
| join kind=inner (
ThreatIntelIndicators
| where ObservableKey contains 'file:hashes'
| where isnotempty(ObservableValue)
| where SourceSystem == "Google Threat Intelligence"
| summarize LatestIndicatorTime = arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by Id
| where IsActive == true and (isnull(ValidUntil) or ValidUntil > now())
) on $left.Hash == $right.ObservableValue
| project Hash, HashType
| extend FileHash_0_Value = Hash
| extend FileHash_0_Algorithm = HashType

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting Hash

properties.description: ThreatHuntIp_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.2.3

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting IP
properties.query:
_Im_NetworkSession
| where isnotempty(DstIpAddr)
| join kind= innerunique  (
ThreatIntelIndicators
| where ObservableKey == 'ipv4-addr:value'
| where isnotempty(ObservableValue)
| where SourceSystem == "Google Threat Intelligence"
| summarize LatestIndicatorTime = arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by Id
| where IsActive == true and (isnull(ValidUntil) or ValidUntil > now())
) on $left.DstIpAddr == $right.ObservableValue
| project DstIpAddr
| extend IP_0_Address = DstIpAddr

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting IP

properties.description: ThreatHuntDomain_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.2.3

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting Domain
properties.query:
_Im_Dns
| where isnotempty(Domain)
| join kind=inner (
ThreatIntelIndicators
| where ObservableKey == 'domain-name:value'
| where isnotempty(ObservableValue)
| where SourceSystem == "Google Threat Intelligence"
| summarize LatestIndicatorTime = arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by Id
| where IsActive == true and (isnull(ValidUntil) or ValidUntil > now())
) on $left.Domain == $right.ObservableValue
| project Domain, DstIpAddr
| extend IP_0_Address = DstIpAddr
| extend DNS_0_DomainName = Domain

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting Domain

properties.description: ThreatHuntUrl_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.2.3

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting Url
properties.query:
_Im_WebSession
| where isnotempty(Url)
| join kind=inner (
ThreatIntelIndicators
| where ObservableKey == 'url:value'
| where isnotempty(ObservableValue)
| where SourceSystem == "Google Threat Intelligence"
| summarize LatestIndicatorTime = arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by Id
| where IsActive == true and (isnull(ValidUntil) or ValidUntil > now())
) on $left.Url == $right.ObservableValue
| project Url
| extend URL_0_Url = Url

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting Url

properties.description: ThreatHuntHash_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.2.3

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting Hash
properties.query:
let ioc_lookBack = 1d;
_Im_FileEvent
| where isnotempty(Hash)
| extend lowerHash=tolower(Hash)
| join kind=inner (
ThreatIntelIndicators
| where ObservableKey contains 'file:hashes'
| where isnotempty(ObservableValue)
| where SourceSystem == "Google Threat Intelligence"
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(ioc_lookBack)
| summarize LatestIndicatorTime = arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by Id
| where IsActive == true and (isnull(ValidUntil) or ValidUntil > now())
| extend lowerHash=tolower(ObservableValue)
) on lowerHash
| project Hash=ObservableValue, HashType=extract("file:hashes.('[^']*')", 1, ObservableKey), Description=Data.description, Type, TimeGenerated

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting Hash

properties.description: ThreatHuntIp_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.2.3

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting IP
properties.query:
let ioc_lookBack = 1d;
_Im_NetworkSession
| where isnotempty(DstIpAddr)
| join kind=inner (
ThreatIntelIndicators
| where ObservableKey == 'ipv4-addr:value'
| where isnotempty(ObservableValue)
| where SourceSystem == "Google Threat Intelligence"
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(ioc_lookBack)
| summarize LatestIndicatorTime = arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by Id
| where IsActive == true and (isnull(ValidUntil) or ValidUntil > now())
) on $left.DstIpAddr == $right.ObservableValue
| project NetworkIP=ObservableValue, Description=Data.description, Type, TimeGenerated

| summarize LatestIndicatorTime = arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by Id
| where IsActive == true and (isnull(ValidUntil) or ValidUntil > now())

) on lowerDomain

_Im_WebSession

) on lowerUrl

properties.displayName: Google Threat Intelligence - Threat Hunting Url

properties.description: GTI_DataLeakAlerts_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.2.3

Triggers an incident when a GTI Relevance System Alert of type data_leak is ingested.
Data Leak alerts indicate that sensitive organisational data (credentials, PII, intellectual property,
source code, databases, etc.) has been found exposed on the dark web, paste sites, or underground
forums and matches your organisation profile.
Each unique Alert ID is grouped into a single incident.
properties.query:
GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts
| where DetailType == "data_leak"
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    AlertId,
    AlertName          = DisplayName,
    AlertState         = State,
    LeakSeverity       = DataLeakSeverity,
    DiscoveryDocuments = DataLeakDiscoveryDocumentIds,
    PriorityLevel,
    PriorityReasoning,
    SeverityLevel,
    SeverityConfidence,
    SeverityReasoning,
    RelevanceLevel,
    RelevanceConfidence,
    RelevanceReasoning,
    FindingCount,
    AiSummary,
    CommonThemes       = RelevanceEvidenceCommonThemes,
    DistinctThemes     = RelevanceEvidenceDistinctThemes,
    AuditCreateTime,
    AuditUpdateTime

properties.description:
Triggers an incident whenever a GTI Relevance System Alert with priority level HIGH or CRITICAL
is ingested. These alerts indicate threats that Google Threat Intelligence has assessed as requiring
immediate attention based on relevance, severity, and potential business impact.
Each unique Alert ID is grouped into a single incident.
properties.displayName: GTI - High and Critical Priority Alerts
properties.query:
GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts
| where PriorityLevel in ("HIGH", "CRITICAL")
| extend
    DetailSeverity = coalesce(DataLeakSeverity, InsiderThreatSeverity, InitialAccessBrokerSeverity, "")
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    AlertId,
    AlertName          = DisplayName,
    AlertState         = State,
    PriorityLevel,
    PriorityReasoning,
    SeverityLevel,
    SeverityConfidence,
    DetailType,
    DetailSeverity,
    RelevanceLevel,
    FindingCount,
    AiSummary,
    AuditCreateTime,
    AuditUpdateTime

properties.displayName: GTI - High and Critical Priority Alerts

properties.description: GTI_HighRelevanceAlerts_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.2.3

Triggers an incident when a GTI Relevance System Alert is assessed as RELEVANCE_LEVEL_HIGH
with a HIGH confidence level. High relevance means Google Threat Intelligence has determined
the threat directly or very closely matches your organisation profile - regardless of threat type.
These are the most actionable alerts and warrant priority investigation.
Each unique Alert ID is grouped into a single incident.
properties.query:
GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts
| where RelevanceLevel == "HIGH"
    and IsRelevant == true
| extend
    DetailSeverity = coalesce(DataLeakSeverity, InsiderThreatSeverity, InitialAccessBrokerSeverity, "")
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    AlertId,
    AlertName           = DisplayName,
    AlertState          = State,
    DetailType,
    DetailSeverity,
    RelevanceLevel,
    RelevanceConfidence,
    RelevanceReasoning,
    CommonThemes        = RelevanceEvidenceCommonThemes,
    DistinctThemes      = RelevanceEvidenceDistinctThemes,
    SeverityLevel,
    SeverityConfidence,
    SeverityReasoning,
    PriorityLevel,
    PriorityReasoning,
    FindingCount,
    AiSummary,
    AuditCreateTime,
    AuditUpdateTime

properties.description:
Triggers an incident when a GTI Relevance System Alert of type initial_access_broker is ingested.
Initial Access Broker alerts indicate that a threat actor is advertising or has sold access
(credentials, VPN, RDP, admin panels, etc.) to an organisation that matches your profile.
These are high-urgency signals that may indicate imminent compromise or an ongoing breach.
Each unique Alert ID is grouped into a single incident.
properties.displayName: GTI - Initial Access Broker Alert Detected
properties.query:
GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts
| where DetailType == "initial_access_broker"
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    AlertId,
    AlertName                       = DisplayName,
    AlertState                      = State,
    BrokerSeverity                  = InitialAccessBrokerSeverity,
    DiscoveryDocuments              = InitialAccessBrokerDiscoveryDocumentIds,
    PriorityLevel,
    PriorityReasoning,
    SeverityLevel,
    SeverityConfidence,
    SeverityReasoning,
    RelevanceLevel,
    RelevanceConfidence,
    RelevanceReasoning,
    FindingCount,
    AiSummary,
    CommonThemes                    = RelevanceEvidenceCommonThemes,
    DistinctThemes                  = RelevanceEvidenceDistinctThemes,
    AuditCreateTime,
    AuditUpdateTime

properties.displayName: GTI - Initial Access Broker Alert Detected

properties.description: GTI_InsiderThreatAlerts_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.2.3

Triggers an incident when a GTI Relevance System Alert of type insider_threat is ingested.
Insider Threat alerts indicate that GTI has identified intelligence suggesting malicious or negligent
activity by individuals with legitimate access to your organisation - such as employee credential
sales, internal data exposure, or rogue employee activity observed in underground sources.
Each unique Alert ID is grouped into a single incident.
properties.query:
GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts
| where DetailType == "insider_threat"
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    AlertId,
    AlertName           = DisplayName,
    AlertState          = State,
    InsiderSeverity     = InsiderThreatSeverity,
    DiscoveryDocuments  = InsiderThreatDiscoveryDocumentIds,
    PriorityLevel,
    PriorityReasoning,
    SeverityLevel,
    SeverityConfidence,
    SeverityReasoning,
    RelevanceLevel,
    RelevanceConfidence,
    RelevanceReasoning,
    FindingCount,
    AiSummary,
    CommonThemes        = RelevanceEvidenceCommonThemes,
    DistinctThemes      = RelevanceEvidenceDistinctThemes,
    AuditCreateTime,
    AuditUpdateTime

properties.description:
Creates a Microsoft Sentinel incident for each unique Google Threat Intelligence (GTI) Alert ID.
Multiple ingestion records sharing the same Alert ID (e.g. updated snapshots of the same alert) are
grouped into a single incident. The rule surfaces the most recent snapshot of each alert and maps
severity from the GTI severity analysis field.
properties.displayName: GTI Relevance System Alert - Incident by Alert ID
properties.query:
GTIRelevanceSystemAlerts
| extend
    SentinelSeverity = case(
        SeverityLevel == "CRITICAL", "High",
        SeverityLevel == "HIGH",     "High",
        SeverityLevel == "MEDIUM",   "Medium",
        SeverityLevel == "LOW",      "Low",
        "Informational"
    ),
    DetailSeverity = coalesce(DataLeakSeverity, InsiderThreatSeverity, InitialAccessBrokerSeverity, "")
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    AlertId,
    AlertName        = DisplayName,
    AlertState       = State,
    DetailType,
    DetailSeverity,
    SeverityLevel,
    SentinelSeverity,
    SeverityConfidence,
    SeverityReasoning,
    PriorityLevel,
    PriorityReasoning,
    RelevanceLevel,
    RelevanceConfidence,
    RelevanceReasoning,
    FindingCount,
    AiSummary,
    AuditCreateTime,
    AuditUpdateTime

properties.displayName: GTI Relevance System Alert - Incident by Alert ID

properties.description: This connector provides access to various Google Threat Intelligence API endpoints for retrieving file, IP, domain, and URL analysis reports.

info.title: Google Threat Intelligence
info.description: This connector provides access to various Google Threat Intelligence API endpoints for retrieving file, IP, domain, and URL analysis reports.

get.description: Retrieve detailed analysis report for a specific file by its ID.

id.description: The unique identifier (SHA-256 hash) of the file.

type.description: The type of object being analyzed (in this case, "file").

self.description: The URL to retrieve this file's report.

times_submitted.description: The number of times this file has been submitted to Google Threat Intelligence.

meaningful_name.description: A human-readable name for the file (if available).

sha1.description: The SHA-1 hash of the file.

magic.description: A textual description of the file type, as determined by libmagic.

harmless.description: The number of votes classifying this file as harmless.
harmless.title: total votes harmless

malicious.description: The number of votes classifying this file as malicious.
malicious.title: total votes malicious

type_tag.description: A high-level categorization of the file type (e.g., "PE executable", "PDF document").

malicious.description: The number of antivirus engines that detected the file as malicious in the latest analysis.
malicious.title: last analysis malicious

suspicious.description: The number of antivirus engines that detected the file as suspicious in the latest analysis.
suspicious.title: last analysis suspicious

undetected.description: The number of antivirus engines that did not detect any threats in the file in the latest analysis.
undetected.title: last analysis undetected

harmless.description: The number of antivirus engines that explicitly classified the file as harmless in the latest analysis.
harmless.title: last analysis harmless

timeout.description: The number of antivirus engines that timed out during the latest analysis.
timeout.title: last analysis timeout

confirmed-timeout.description: The number of antivirus engines that experienced a confirmed timeout during the latest analysis.
confirmed-timeout.title: last analysis confirmed-timeout

failure.description: The number of antivirus engines that encountered an error during the latest analysis.
failure.title: last analysis failure

type-unsupported.description: The number of antivirus engines that do not support the analysis of this file type.
type-unsupported.title: last analysis type-unsupported

ssdeep.description: The ssdeep fuzzy hash of the file, used for similarity comparison.

type_description.description: A more detailed description of the file type (e.g., "Microsoft Word Document").

tlsh.description: The TLSH fuzzy hash of the file, used for similarity comparison.

reputation.description: The reputation score of the file, calculated based on various factors.

sha256.description: The SHA-256 hash of the file.

unique_sources.description: The number of unique sources from which this file has been submitted.

names.description: A list of names associated with the file.

tags.description: Tags associated with the file, providing additional context or categorization.

last_submission_date.description: The Unix timestamp of the last time this file was submitted to Google Threat Intelligence.

last_modification_date.description: The Unix timestamp of the last modification date of the file (if available).

size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

md5.description: The MD5 hash of the file.

value.description: The severity level assigned by Google Threat Intelligence's GTI (e.g., "SEVERITY_NONE", "SEVERITY_LOW", etc.).
value.title: severity

severity.description: The severity assessment of the file.

value.description: The threat score assigned by Google Threat Intelligence's GTI, indicating the likelihood of the file being malicious.
value.title: threat score

threat_score.description: The threat score assessment of the file.

value.description: The overall verdict of the file's analysis by Google Threat Intelligence's GTI (e.g., "VERDICT_UNDETECTED", "VERDICT_MALICIOUS", etc.).
value.title: verdict

verdict.description: The overall verdict of the file's analysis.

description.description: A textual description of the GTI assessment.

gti_assessment.description: GTI's (Google Threat Intelligence) assessment of the file.

attributes.description: Attributes and metadata associated with the file.

get.description: Retrieve analysis and reputation information about a specific IP address.

id.description: The unique identifier for this IP address report.

self.description: The URL to retrieve this IP address report.

asn.description: The Autonomous System Number (ASN) associated with the IP address.

network.description: The network or CIDR block the IP address belongs to.

last_https_certificate_date.description: The date of the last HTTPS certificate seen for this IP address (Unix timestamp).

country.description: The country associated with the IP address.

as_owner.description: The name of the organization that owns the ASN associated with the IP address.

reputation.description: The reputation score of the IP address (-100 to 100).

harmless.description: The number of votes classifying the IP address as harmless.
harmless.title: total votes harmless

malicious.description: The number of votes classifying the IP address as malicious.
malicious.title: total votes malicious

total_votes.description: The breakdown of votes on the IP address's maliciousness.

whois.description: The raw WHOIS data for the IP address.

tags.description: Tags associated with the IP address based on community analysis.

malicious.description: The number of engines that detected the IP address as malicious.
malicious.title: last analysis malicious

suspicious.description: The number of engines that detected the IP address as suspicious.
suspicious.title: last analysis suspicious

undetected.description: The number of engines that did not detect the IP address as malicious or suspicious.
undetected.title: last analysis undetected

harmless.description: The number of engines that detected the IP address as harmless.
harmless.title: last analysis harmless

timeout.description: The number of engines that timed out during the analysis of the IP address.
timeout.title: last analysis timeout

last_analysis_stats.description: Statistics from the last analysis of the IP address.

whois_date.description: The date the WHOIS data was last updated.

regional_internet_registry.description: The Regional Internet Registry (RIR) responsible for the IP address.

last_modification_date.description: The date the IP address report was last modified.

continent.description: The continent where the IP address is located.

value.description: The severity level assigned by GTI.
value.title: severity

severity.description: The severity level assigned by GTI.

value.description: The threat score assigned by GTI (0-100).
value.title: threat score

threat_score.description: The threat score assigned by GTI (0-100).

mandiant_confidence_score.description: The confidence score assigned by Mandiant (0-100).

normalised_categories.description: Normalized categories associated with the IP address.

pervasive_indicator.description: Indicates if the IP address is a pervasive indicator.

safebrowsing_verdict.description: The verdict from Google Safe Browsing.

contributing_factors.description: Factors contributing to the GTI assessment.

description.description: A description of the GTI assessment.

gti_assessment.description: Google Threat Intelligence (GTI) assessment of the IP address.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: Ip address to obtain the report

get.description: Retrieve analysis and reputation information about a specific domain.

self.description: The url to retrieve this domain report.

harmless.description: The count of votes indicating the domain is safe.
harmless.title: total votes harmless

malicious.description: The count of votes suggesting the domain is harmful.
malicious.title: total votes malicious

total_votes.description: his object provides a summary of community votes regarding the domain's potential maliciousness.

reputation.description: A numerical score ranging from -100 to 100, reflecting the domain's overall reputation based on various factors.

whois.description: The raw WHOIS data associated with the domain, containing registration details and contact information.

last_https_certificate_date.description: The Unix timestamp indicating when the last HTTPS certificate for this domain was observed.

last_dns_records_date.description: The Unix timestamp representing the last time DNS records were fetched for this domain.

registrar.description: The entity responsible for registering the domain name.

last_modification_date.description: The Unix timestamp showing when the domain report was last updated.

creation_date.description: The Unix timestamp marking the domain's initial registration date.

tags.description: An array of tags assigned to the domain based on community analysis and observations.

last_update_date.description: The Unix timestamp indicating the most recent update to the domain's information.

malicious.description: The number of analysis engines flagging the domain as malicious.
malicious.title: last analysis malicious

suspicious.description: The count of engines raising suspicion about the domain.
suspicious.title: last analysis suspicious

undetected.description: The number of engines that didn't detect any malicious or suspicious activity.
undetected.title: last analysis undetected

harmless.description: The count of engines explicitly classifying the domain as safe.
harmless.title: last analysis harmless

timeout.description: The number of engines that timed out during the analysis.
timeout.title: last analysis timeout

last_analysis_stats.description: Statistics derived from the latest analysis of the domain.

value.description: The severity level assigned by GTI.
value.title: severity

severity.description: The severity level assigned by GTI.

value.description: The threat score assigned by GTI (0-100).
value.title: threat score

threat_score.description: The threat score assigned by GTI (0-100).

mandiant_confidence_score.description: The confidence score assigned by Mandiant (0-100).

normalised_categories.description: Normalized categories associated with the IP address.

pervasive_indicator.description: Indicates if the IP address is a pervasive indicator.

safebrowsing_verdict.description: The verdict from Google Safe Browsing.

contributing_factors.description: Factors contributing to the GTI assessment.

description.description: A description of the GTI assessment.

gti_assessment.description: Google Threat Intelligence (GTI) assessment of the IP address.

get.description: Retrieve analysis and reputation information about a specific URL.

type.description: The type of object being analyzed

self.description: Url to retrieve this url report

last_final_url.description: The final URL after any redirects.

harmless.description: Number of votes indicating the URL is safe.
harmless.title: total votes harmless

malicious.description: Number of votes indicating the URL is harmful.
malicious.title: total votes malicious

total_votes.description: The breakdown of votes from the Google Threat Intelligence community on whether the URL is considered harmless or malicious.

tags.description: Community-assigned tags providing additional context or categorization for the URL.

times_submitted.description: The total number of times this URL has been submitted to Google Threat Intelligence for analysis.

last_modification_date.description: The date (in Unix timestamp format) when the URL report was last modified.

reputation.description: A score ranging from -100 to 100, representing the URL's reputation based on various factors and community assessments.

last_analysis_date.description: The date (in Unix timestamp format) when the last analysis of the URL was performed.

malicious.description: The number of engines that detected the URL as malicious.

suspicious.description: The number of engines that flagged the URL as suspicious.

undetected.description: The number of engines that did not detect any threats in the URL.

url.description: The actual URL that was analyzed.

first_submission_date.description: The date (in Unix timestamp format) of the very first time this URL was submitted to Google Threat Intelligence.

title.description: The title or webpage name extracted from the URL

severity.description: The severity level assigned by GTI.

value.description: The threat score assigned by GTI (0-100).

contributing_factors.description: Factors contributing to the GTI assessment.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: URL id to obtain the report

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: The file to upload for analysis

get.summary: Retrieve information about a file or URL analysis
get.description: Get the status and results of a file or URL analysis.

id.description: The unique identifier for this analysis request.

type.description: Indicates the type of analysis performed, either "file" or "url".

self.description: The URL to retrieve the analysis information itself.

item.description: The URL to access the analyzed file or URL.

links.description: Provides URLs related to the analysis.

malicious.description: The number of engines that flagged the item as malicious.

suspicious.description: The number of engines that flagged the item as malicious.

undetected.description: The number of engines that didn't detect any threats.

harmless.description: The number of engines that explicitly classified the item as harmless.

timeout.description: The number of engines that timed out during the analysis.

confirmed-timeout.description: The number of engines that experienced a confirmed timeout.

failure.description: The number of engines that encountered an error during analysis.

type-unsupported.description: The number of engines that don't support analyzing this type of item.

stats.description: Summarizes the detection results from various antivirus engines.

date.description: The date (in Unix timestamp format) when the analysis was completed.

status.description: The current status of the analysis.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Analysis id to obtain the report

get.description: Obtain list of threats given the category of the threats and the timestamp of the desired hour.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter to the threat list search

get.description: Obtain list of threats given the source and the timestamp of the desired hour.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter to the ioc stream search

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of iocs returned

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor to the next batch of iocs

inputs.name: Comments
inputs.value:
GTI IP Report


IP: @{body('Get_IP_Report')?['data']?['id']}
Reputation is: @{body('Get_IP_Report')?['data']?['attributes']?['reputation']}

GTI Assessment


- Score: @{body('Get_IP_Report')?['data']?['attributes']?['gti_assessment']?['threat_score']?['value']}
- Verdict:
- Severity: @{body('Get_IP_Report')?['data']?['attributes']?['gti_assessment']?['severity']?['value']}

inputs.name: Comments
inputs.value:
GTI File Report


Hash: @{body('Get_File_Report')?['data']?['id']}
Reputation is: @{body('Get_File_Report')?['data']?['attributes']?['reputation']}


GTI Assessment


-Score: @{body('Get_File_Report')?['data']?['attributes']?['gti_assessment']?['threat_score']?['value']}
-Verdict: @{body('Get_File_Report')?['data']?['attributes']?['gti_assessment']?['verdict']?['value']}
-Severity: @{body('Get_File_Report')?['data']?['attributes']?['gti_assessment']?['severity']?['value']}

inputs.name: Comments
inputs.value:

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

This Google Threat Intelligence Solution contains Playbooks that can help enrich incident information with threat information and intelligence for IPs, file hashes and URLs from Google Threat Intelligence. Enriched information can help drive focused investigations in Security Operations.

Important — Custom Connector prerequisite: The Playbooks in this solution depend on the Google Threat Intelligence custom Logic Apps connector, which is not deployed automatically when you install the solution from Content Hub. Before running any of the Playbooks, you must manually deploy the custom connector into the same resource group and region as the Playbooks, using the Deploy to Azure button in the connector's readme. Without it, the Playbooks will fail to authenticate to the Google Threat Intelligence API.

Data Connectors: 1, Parsers: 1, Analytic Rules: 10, Hunting Queries: 4, Custom Azure Logic Apps Connectors: 1, Playbooks: 8

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
